expectations.text: Reads existing context file before making changes (check if existing file was created and updated file differs from it)
expectations.evidence: existing-game-marketing-context.md exists (78 lines, original content). Updated game-marketing-context.md differs substantially — 95 lines, new CPI actuals columns, retention actuals column, Campaign History section, and revised age range. File was clearly read and then updated rather than created from scratch.

expectations.text: Updates CPI actuals: US $1.80 and Germany $0.95
expectations.evidence: Updated file CPI table includes: '| US | $1.50 | $1.80 | Over target — optimize |' and '| DE | $1.00 | $0.95 | On target — scale |'

expectations.text: Notes D7 retention actual (18%) vs target (22%) gap
expectations.evidence: Retention table shows '| D7 | 22% | 18% | Below target (-4pp) |' and notes section states 'D7 at 18% is 4 percentage points below the 22% target.'

expectations.text: Updates target player age range to 25-40 (not 18-35)
expectations.evidence: Updated file shows '**Age Range**: 25–40 *(updated from 18–35 — first campaign data shows primary skew is 25–40)*'

expectations.text: Does not rewrite unrelated sections (monetization, store presence, brand voice should be essentially unchanged)
expectations.evidence: Monetization line in Product Overview is identical. UA Channels, Messaging Pillars, and Localization Notes sections are word-for-word unchanged between files. Creative Strategy gained one line about audience refinement but core content is preserved. No brand voice or store presence sections existed to be rewritten.

A 'Campaign History' table was added summarizing Wave 1 findings: 'US CPI $1.80 (over); DE CPI $0.95 (on target); D7 retention 18% (below 22% target); audience skews 25–40 male'. Open Questions checkmarks also explicitly confirm what was validated.
